java.answer: Loop starts at 0 and goes to 4. Should be i=1; i<=5 to print 1 to 5.

c.answer: Loop starts at 0 and goes to 4. Should be i=1; i<=5.

title: Wrong Comparison Operator
description: The code should check if a equals b, but it always prints 'equal'. Fix it.

python.answer: Change a = b to a == b. Single = is assignment, double == is comparison.

java.answer: Change a = b to a == b. Also, a=b assigns b to a, which is non-zero, treated as true.

c.answer: a = b assigns 10 to a. The result 10 is truthy. Change to a == b.

java.code: Lines: ["int b = 10;", "a = b;", "int temp = a;", "int a = 5;", "b = temp;", "System.out.println(a + \" \" + b);"]

c.answer: int a = 5; → int b = 10; → int temp = a; → a = b; → b = temp; → printf

python.code: Lines: ["n = 4", "result *= i", "result = 1", "print(result)", "for i in range(1, n+1):"]
python.answer: n = 4 → result = 1 → for i in range(1, n+1): → result *= i → print(result)

java.code: Lines: ["int n = 4;", "result *= i;", "int result = 1;", "System.out.println(result);", "for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {", "}"]

c.code: Lines: ["int n = 4;", "result *= i;", "int result = 1;", "printf(\"%d\\n\", result);", "for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++) {", "}"]
c.answer: int n = 4; → int result = 1; → for(...){ → result *= i; → } → printf

title: Off-by-One in Binary Search
description: This binary search has a bug. Find and fix it.

python.answer: hi should be len(arr) - 1, not len(arr). Current code causes index out of range when mid = len(arr).

java.answer: hi should be arr.length - 1. Then condition becomes lo <= hi.

c.answer: hi should be n - 1. Condition becomes lo <= hi.

title: Short-Circuit Evaluation Side Effects
description: What is printed?

python.code:
def f():
    print('f called')
    return False

def g():
    print('g called')
    return True

result = f() and g()
print(result)
python.answer:
f called
False
python.explanation: Short-circuit: f() is False, so g() is never called

java.answer:
f called
false

c.answer:
f called
0
c.explanation: Short-circuit &&: f() returns 0, g() never runs

java.answer: 7 and true

c.answer: 7 and 1

title: Bubble Sort Pass
description: Arrange lines to complete one pass of bubble sort.

python.code: Lines: ["arr = [5, 3, 8, 1]", "for i in range(len(arr)-1):", "    arr[i], arr[i+1] = arr[i+1], arr[i]", "    if arr[i] > arr[i+1]:", "print(arr)"]
python.answer: arr = [...] → for i in range(len(arr)-1): → if arr[i] > arr[i+1]: → arr[i], arr[i+1] = swap → print

java.code: Lines: ["int[] arr = {5,3,8,1};", "for (int i=0; i<arr.length-1; i++) {", "if (arr[i] > arr[i+1]) {", "int tmp = arr[i]; arr[i]=arr[i+1]; arr[i+1]=tmp;", "} }", "System.out.println(Arrays.toString(arr));"]
java.answer: int[] arr → for loop → if check → swap → close braces → print

c.code: Lines: ["int arr[] = {5,3,8,1};", "for (int i=0; i<3; i++) {", "if (arr[i] > arr[i+1]) {", "int tmp = arr[i]; arr[i]=arr[i+1]; arr[i+1]=tmp;", "} }", "for (int i=0;i<4;i++) printf(\"%d \",arr[i]);"]
c.answer: int arr[] → for loop → if check → swap → close braces → print

title: Scope Error Inside Nested Function
description: Identify the error.

python.code:
def outer():
    x = 10
    def inner():
        x += 1
        print(x)
    inner()

outer()
python.answer: UnboundLocalError: x += 1 tries to read x before assigning in inner scope. Need 'nonlocal x' inside inner().

java.answer: Error: local variable x accessed from inner class must be effectively final. Cannot do x++ inside anonymous class.

c.answer: Nested functions are not standard C. GCC extension only. Not portable. Standard C has no nested functions.

java.answer: HELLO hello
java.explanation: Strings are immutable in Java; toUpperCase returns new String

c.answer: HELLO hello
c.explanation: t was copied before modification; s is modified in-place

title: Wrong String Concatenation

int age = 25;
printf("Age: " + age);

title: Stack Implementation Bug
description: The pop function has a bug. Fix it.

python.answer: pop() returns items[0] (bottom), not the top. Should return self.items.pop() or self.items[-1] and remove it.

java.answer: get(0) returns the first (bottom) item. Should be items.remove(items.size()-1).

c.answer: pop() returns stack[0] (bottom). Should return stack[top--] to return and remove top element.

print('-'.join(parts[::-1]))
python.answer: d-c-b-a
python.explanation: Split by comma → ['a','b','c','d'], reversed → ['d','c','b','a'], joined with '-'

java.answer: d-c-b-a
java.explanation: Split, reverse iterate, join with dash

c.answer: d-c-b-a

title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume exactly one solution exists.
input_format: nums: list of integers, target: integer
output_format: List/array of two indices [i, j] where i < j

title: Reverse String
description: Write a function that reverses a string. The input is given as an array of characters. Modify it in-place with O(1) extra memory.
output_format: Modify in place, no return value

title: Palindrome Number
description: Given an integer x, return true if x is a palindrome, false otherwise. A palindrome reads the same forward and backward.
constraints: -2^31 <= x <= 2^31 - 1. Negative numbers are NOT palindromes.

python.answer:
def is_palindrome(x):
    if x < 0: return False
    s = str(x)
    return s == s[::-1]

title: Count Character Frequency
description: Given a string, return a dictionary/map of each character and how many times it appears.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 10^5, s consists of printable ASCII characters

title: Find Maximum in Array
description: Given an unsorted array of integers, return the maximum value.

title: Check Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s.
constraints: 1 <= s.length, t.length <= 5*10^4. Lowercase English letters only.

title: Missing Number
description: Given an array nums containing n distinct numbers in the range [0, n], return the only number in the range that is missing.
input_format: nums: array of n distinct integers in [0,n]

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing only '(', ')', '{', '}', '[', ']', determine if the input string is valid. A string is valid if brackets are closed in the correct order.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 10^4. s consists of brackets only.

title: Rotate Array
description: Given an integer array nums, rotate the array to the right by k steps. Modify in-place.

python.answer:
def rotate(nums, k):
    n = len(nums)
    k %= n
    nums[:] = nums[-k:] + nums[:-k]

title: Find Duplicate in Array
description: Given an array of n+1 integers in range [1, n], find the duplicate number. There is exactly one duplicate.
constraints: 1 <= n <= 10^5, nums contains exactly one duplicate

title: Second Largest Element
description: Given an array of integers, find the second largest unique element. Return -1 if it does not exist.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 10^4, all elements fit in int

title: Count Vowels in String
description: Given a string, count the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u — both uppercase and lowercase).

python.answer:
def count_vowels(s):
    return sum(1 for c in s if c.lower() in 'aeiou')

title: Fibonacci Sequence
description: Given n, return the nth Fibonacci number (0-indexed: fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1).

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
description: Given a sorted integer array nums, remove duplicates in-place. Return the count of unique elements.
output_format: integer k (count of unique values); first k elements of nums are the unique values
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 3*10^4, nums is sorted in non-decreasing order

title: Longest Common Prefix
description: Write a function to find the longest common prefix string among an array of strings. Return empty string if there is none.
constraints: 1 <= strs.length <= 200, 0 <= strs[i].length <= 200. All lowercase English letters.

title: Count Pairs with Given Sum
description: Given an array of integers and a target sum, count all unique pairs (i, j) with i < j such that nums[i] + nums[j] == target.
input_format: nums: array of integers, target: integer

title: Check Sorted Array
description: Given an array, return true if it is sorted in non-decreasing order, false otherwise.

title: Intersection of Two Arrays
description: Given two integer arrays, return an array of their intersection (common unique elements).
output_format: array of unique common elements (any order)

python.answer:
def intersection(nums1, nums2):
    return list(set(nums1) & set(nums2))

title: Sum of Digits
description: Given a non-negative integer n, repeatedly sum its digits until the result is a single digit. Return that single digit.

title: Left Rotate Array by K
description: Given an array and integer k, left-rotate the array by k positions. Modify in-place.
input_format: nums: array of integers, k: integer (0 <= k < nums.length)

python.answer:
def left_rotate(nums, k):

python.code:
def has_pair(arr, target):
    arr.sort()
    left, right = 0, len(arr)
    while left < right:
        s = arr[left] + arr[right]
        if s == target: return True
        elif s < target: left += 1
        else: right -= 1
    return False

print(has_pair([1,4,6,8], 10))
python.answer: right should start at len(arr)-1, not len(arr). arr[len(arr)] is out of bounds.

java.answer: right = arr.length causes ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException. Fix: right = arr.length - 1.

c.answer: right = n is out of bounds. Fix: right = n - 1.

title: Wrong Carry in Addition
description: This manual digit addition has a logic error. Find it.

python.code:
def add_strings(a, b):
    i, j, carry = len(a)-1, len(b)-1, 0
    result = []
    while i >= 0 or j >= 0:
        x = int(a[i]) if i >= 0 else 0
        y = int(b[j]) if j >= 0 else 0
        total = x + y + carry
        result.append(str(total % 10))
        carry = total // 10
        i -= 1; j -= 1
    return ''.join(reversed(result))

print(add_strings('99', '1'))
python.answer: Missing: after while loop, if carry > 0: result.append(str(carry)). Output is '00' instead of '100'.

java.answer: Missing: if (carry>0) res.append(carry); after while loop. Without it, carry from last digit is lost.

c.code: // Same logic — missing carry append after loop
c.answer: After the while loop, add: if (carry) { result[k++] = '0' + carry; } before reversing the result array.

title: Compress String (Run-Length Encoding)
description: Implement basic string compression: replace consecutive repeated characters with character + count. If compressed is not shorter, return original.

python.answer:
def compress(s):
    if not s: return s
    result, i = [], 0
    while i < len(s):
        char, count = s[i], 1
        while i+count < len(s) and s[i+count] == char:
            count += 1
        result.append(char if count==1 else char+str(count))
        i += count
    compressed = ''.join(result)
    return compressed if len(compressed) < len(s) else s

title: Product of Array Except Self (Brute Force)
description: Given an array, return an array where each element is the product of all other elements. Do not use division.

python.answer:
def product_except_self(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    res = [1] * n
    prefix = 1
    for i in range(n):
        res[i] = prefix
        prefix *= nums[i]
    suffix = 1
    for i in range(n-1, -1, -1):
        res[i] *= suffix
        suffix *= nums[i]
    return res

title: Subarray Sum Equals K (Count)
description: Given an array and integer k, return the total number of continuous subarrays whose sum equals k.
input_format: nums: array of integers, k: integer

python.answer:
def subarray_sum(nums, k):
    from collections import defaultdict
    count, prefix = 0, 0
    freq = defaultdict(int)
    freq[0] = 1
    for n in nums:
        prefix += n
        count += freq[prefix - k]
        freq[prefix] += 1
    return count

title: Linked List Cycle Detection
description: Given the head of a linked list, return true if it has a cycle (Floyd's algorithm).

title: Find Numbers With Even Number of Digits
description: Given an array of integers, return how many of them contain an even number of digits.

python.answer:
def find_numbers(nums):
    return sum(1 for n in nums if len(str(n)) % 2 == 0)

title: Maximum Consecutive Ones
description: Given a binary array nums, return the maximum number of consecutive 1's.

title: Find Pivot Index
description: Return the leftmost pivot index where the sum of elements to the left equals the sum to the right. Return -1 if none exists.

title: Check If Array Is Sorted and Rotated
description: Given an array, return true if it was originally sorted in non-decreasing order and then possibly rotated.

python.answer:
def check(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    drops = sum(1 for i in range(n) if nums[i] > nums[(i+1)%n])
    return drops <= 1

title: Richest Customer Wealth
description: Given an m x n grid accounts where accounts[i][j] is the amount of money the i-th customer has in the j-th bank, return the wealth of the richest customer.

python.answer:
def maximum_wealth(accounts):
    return max(sum(row) for row in accounts)

title: Replace Elements With Greatest on Right
description: Replace every element with the greatest element among all elements to its right. Replace the last element with -1.

python.answer:
def replace_elements(arr):
    max_right = -1
    for i in range(len(arr)-1, -1, -1):
        new_max = max(max_right, arr[i])
        arr[i] = max_right
        max_right = new_max
    return arr

title: Squares of Sorted Array
description: Given an integer array sorted in non-decreasing order, return an array of the squares of each number, also in non-decreasing order.

title: Third Maximum Number
description: Given an array, return the third distinct maximum. If it does not exist, return the maximum.

title: String to Integer (atoi)
description: Implement atoi: parse leading whitespace, optional +/- sign, then digits. Stop at first non-digit. Clamp to 32-bit int range.

python.answer:
def my_atoi(s):
    s = s.lstrip()
    if not s: return 0
    sign = -1 if s[0]=='-' else 1
    if s[0] in '+-': s = s[1:]
    num = 0
    for c in s:
        if not c.isdigit(): break
        num = num*10 + int(c)
    num *= sign
    return max(-(2**31), min(2**31-1, num))

title: Longest Palindromic Substring (Brute Force)
description: Given a string, return the longest palindromic substring. If tie, return first.
